rulings.Case Title: U.S. BANK N.A. (F/K/A MUFG UNION BANK, NA) VS. TOD SCHLESINGER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2025, line 1, PETITIONER U.S. BANK N.A. (F/K/A MUFG UNION BANK, NA) Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Off calendar. A request for dismissal of entire action was filed on 2/06/2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org wieth a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DANYELL SANDERS VS. KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal Without Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2025, line 2, PLAINTIFF DANYELL SANDERS' , ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED Motion For Dismissal Without Prejudice The matter is continued to March 6, 2025. Counsel for plaintiff is directed to file a supplemental declaration in support of its motion attaching the settlement agreement in Halfhill v. Kaiser Foundation Hospitals, Case No. R621096610 (Alameda County Superior Court) at least five court days before the continued hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: DANIEL POWELL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Motion To Compel Discovery; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2025, line 4, PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL'S Motion To Compel Discovery Plaintiff Daniel Powell's motion to compel discovery is denied. Plaintiff failed to set forth specific facts showing good cause for the discovery. (CCP 2031.310(b)(1).) Defendants' request for a protective order is denied without prejudice to a properly noticed motion. (CCP 2031.060(a).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Arbitration And Request For Order Staying Proceedings As To All Parties Pending Completion Of Arbitration (Joinder)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2025, line 5, 2 - DEFENDANT JAMES NADEAU, LISA NADEAU Motion To Compel Arbitration And Request For Order Staying Proceedings As To All Parties Pending Completion Of Arbitration (Joinder) The Nadeaus' joinder to the motion to stay is denied as moot.
